title: Approved Intake Brief Handoff for Brownfield Access Review Modernization
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-intake-brief.md` and `./access-review-modernization-discovery-notes.md`. Work only from those two files. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, label them clearly and continue. Produce a first-release requirements set that preserves the handoff constraints, brownfield coexistence boundary, risks, and open questions from intake.

assertions.name: stays-in-specification-phase
assertions.description: Output remains in the requirements layer and does not jump into architecture, cutover planning, or implementation design

assertions.name: preserves-brownfield-boundary
assertions.description: Keeps the release-1 coexistence constraint with the legacy module instead of assuming a full rewrite or same-day cutover

assertions.name: preserves-intake-risks
assertions.description: Carries at least one intake risk or ambiguity into the resulting requirements/open questions

assertions.name: preserves-intake-open-questions
assertions.description: Keeps at least one intake open question unresolved rather than silently deciding it

assertions.name: respects-no-invented-precision
assertions.description: Does not convert unsupported qualitative expectations such as 'feel faster' or 'same-day sync' into hard numerical targets without labeling them as assumptions or open questions

assertions.name: prepares-downstream-handoff
assertions.description: Produces an artifact shaped for downstream system-design or acceptance-criteria work
